channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18468] Md5 chksum fails on the mirror file created by dd with bs=4k after doing mirror resync and delete
item.description:
I hit this issue when I am working on the patch of LU-13720, in sanity.sh test_211. 
If bs=4k (probably multiple of 4K), md5 chksum always fails on the mirror file created by dd after doing mirror resync and delete.

It can be easily reproduced by the following scripts:

# dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/abc bs=4k count=10 oflag=direct
# lfs mirror extend -N /mnt/lustre/abc
# dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/abc bs=4k count=1 oflag=direct
# md5sum /mnt/lustre/abc
620f0b67a91f7f74151bc5be745b7110  /mnt/lustre/abc

# lfs mirror resync /mnt/lustre/abc
# lfs mirror delete --mirror-id=1 /mnt/lustre/abc
# md5sum /mnt/lustre/abc
ab893875d697a3145af5eed5309bee26  /mnt/lustre/abc

Md5 check can pass if bs is set to other than multiple of 4k, e.g. 1k.
item.summary: Md5 chksum fails on the mirror file created by dd with bs=4k after doing mirror resync and delete

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58122/
Subject: LU-18468 tests: write PAGE_SIZE for sanity-flr/211
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 858af0a95584f9dd8ff0ff7310637bb06e70f06d

"Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58122
Subject: LU-18468 tests: write PAGE_SIZE for sanity-flr/211
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 67fb1090a2f42d2fb88617738ffad305589db09f

"Etienne AUJAMES <eaujames@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57668
Subject: LU-18468 llapi: always truncate mirrors on resync
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 16b8ffd7171d597943d0210ac8e41e6db82c1d44
Merged for 2.17

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57090/
Subject: LU-18468 llapi: always truncate mirrors on resync
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: b5db891cad71f17dda00ab157332db819d2292f8

So I think before doing the mirror resync/copy, the file size should be retrieved,

it would be very helpful if you could construct a testcase for this or show an example of layout.

bobijam would it be OK to:

  • remove lod_check_layout_gen_intrans() from lod_save_layout_gen_intrans()
  • save current generation in lod_trans_start()

hmm, I'd think we shouldn't change layout generation at declaration? i.e. we save current generation before all needed locking is taken (say, at/before trans start), then we grab locks and then verify the generations haven't changed otherwise restart?

Specifically, I get that "cannot get UNLOCK lease", does anyone else see that?

that's because two layout declarations within a single transaction:

		/**
		 * declare delete mirror objects in @buf_vic, will change obj's
		 * in-memory layout
		 */
		rc = mdd_declare_xattr_set(env, mdd, obj, buf_vic,
					   XATTR_NAME_LOV, LU_XATTR_PURGE,
					   handle);
		if (rc)
			GOTO(stop, rc);

		/* declare obj set remaining layout in @buf */
		rc = mdd_declare_xattr_set(env, mdd, obj, buf,
					   XATTR_NAME_LOV, LU_XATTR_SPLIT,
					   handle);
yes, LU-18466 would fix this (lod_save_layout_gen_intrans() error)

Specifically, I get that "cannot get UNLOCK lease", does anyone else see that?

yes, this is due to:

00000004:80000000:0.0:1732163050.889192:0:5347:0:(lod_internal.h:471:lod_save_layout_gen_intrans()) lustre-MDT0000-mdtlov: obj ffff9cb4cceac770 gen changed from 4 to 5 in trans declaration
00000004:00000001:0.0:1732163050.889193:0:5347:0:(lod_object.c:3858:lod_declare_xattr_set()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551594 : -22 : ffffffffffffffea)

bobijam can you please comment on this?

iirc, I notice this in the customer's logs quite often.

The final pos value is the position where source mirror contains data, it does not mean the end of file, for example, if the last components of all the mirrors are up-to-date,  the final pos would be the most further position of the extent end of all stale components to be resync-ed.

So I think before doing the mirror resync/copy, the file size should be retrieved, 

if (comp_array[i].lrc_synced) {
   if (pos >= comp_array[i].lrc_start && pos < comp_array[i].lrc_end) {
          if (pos < file_size)
             llapi_mirror_punch(fd, comp_array[i].lrc_mirror_id, comp_array[i].lrc_end - pos);
          else
             llapi_mirror_truncate(fd, comp_array[i].lrc_mirror_id, pos);
    ....
}

"Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57090
Subject: LU-18468 llapi: always truncate mirrors on resync
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 4267a993e16e4e1419519e3c399274f855d8c396
sanity-flr passed twice with the patch above, gonna stress it a bit more..

this one does better, but this is a code I haven't touched before....:

@@ -3523,7 +3533,9 @@ do_read:
         */
        for (i = 0; i < comp_size; i++) {
                comp_array[i].lrc_synced = !comp_array[i].lrc_synced;
-               if (comp_array[i].lrc_synced && pos & (page_size - 1)) {
+               if (pos < comp_array[i].lrc_start || pos >= comp_array[i].lrc_end)
+                       continue;
+               if (comp_array[i].lrc_synced) {
                        rc = llapi_mirror_truncate(fd,
                                        comp_array[i].lrc_mirror_id, pos);
                        /* Ignore truncate error on encrypted file without the

the patch above fails sanity-flr/41 with:

[   34.724835] LustreError: 7895:0:(lov_io.c:650:lov_io_slice_init()) [0x200000401:0x3:0x0]: trying to instantiate components for designated I/O, file state: 3

because one of component was not initialized after resync (it's just not needed):

/mnt/lustre/f41.sanity-flr
  lcm_layout_gen:    8
  lcm_mirror_count:  2
  lcm_entry_count:   7
    lcme_id:             65537
    lcme_mirror_id:      1
    lcme_flags:          init
    lcme_extent.e_start: 0
    lcme_extent.e_end:   2097152
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: 2
      lmm_objects:
      -   0: { l_ost_idx:   2, l_fid: [0x280000400:0x2:0x0] }

    lcme_id:             65538
    lcme_mirror_id:      1
    lcme_flags:          0
    lcme_extent.e_start: 2097152
    lcme_extent.e_end:   4194304
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: -1

    lcme_id:             65539
    lcme_mirror_id:      1
    lcme_flags:          0
    lcme_extent.e_start: 4194304
    lcme_extent.e_end:   EOF
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: -1

    lcme_id:             131076
    lcme_mirror_id:      2
    lcme_flags:          init,stale
    lcme_extent.e_start: 0
    lcme_extent.e_end:   1048576
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: 3
      lmm_objects:
      -   0: { l_ost_idx:   3, l_fid: [0x2c0000400:0x2:0x0] }

    lcme_id:             131077
    lcme_mirror_id:      2
    lcme_flags:          stale
    lcme_extent.e_start: 1048576
    lcme_extent.e_end:   2097152
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: -1

    lcme_id:             131078
    lcme_mirror_id:      2
    lcme_flags:          0
    lcme_extent.e_start: 2097152
    lcme_extent.e_end:   3145728
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: -1

    lcme_id:             131079
    lcme_mirror_id:      2
    lcme_flags:          0
    lcme_extent.e_start: 3145728
    lcme_extent.e_end:   EOF
      lmm_stripe_count:  1
      lmm_stripe_size:   1048576
      lmm_pattern:       raid0
      lmm_layout_gen:    0
      lmm_stripe_offset: -1

This was introduced when the FLR feature was addd, in llapi_mirror_copy, then that code was copied for llapi_mirror_resync_many_params, which replaced llapi_mirror_copy in the lfs mirror resync usage.  But the bug was already present in llapi_mirror_copy, so it has been there since the first FLR release.

The patch which added the problematic check:

    LU-9771 flr: resync support and test tool

    A tool to resync mirrored file after writing.
    It extends the Lustre lease API to support taking file lease and then
    sending the MDS_REINT_RESYNC RPC to the MDT so that it can increase
    the file's layout version; then the client will start copying
    the contents from valid mirror to stale mirrors. At the end of
    resync, the copying client will release the lease and revalidate
    stale mirrors.

    Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-flr
    Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
    Change-Id: I59f84cd501f6945225c97bdb99a142ae7efbf0fb
    Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/29096

When I do this on master, I can reproduce the problem, but I get an additional error - added ls -la for clarity on size:

[root@rocky801 lustre]# lfs setstripe -E -1 /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 40960 Nov 20 14:11 /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# lfs mirror extend -N /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 40960 Nov 20 14:11 /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/abc bs=4k count=1 oflag=direct
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
4096 bytes (4.1 kB, 4.0 KiB) copied, 0.00365505 s, 1.1 MB/s
[root@rocky801 lustre]# ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 4096 Nov 20 14:11 /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# md5sum /mnt/lustre/abc
620f0b67a91f7f74151bc5be745b7110  /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# lfs mirror resync /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 4096 Nov 20 14:11 /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# lfs mirror delete --mirror-id=1 /mnt/lustre/abc
lfs mirror mirror: cannot get UNLOCK lease, ext 8 for [0x200000401:0x9:0x0]: Invalid argument (22)
[root@rocky801 lustre]# ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 40960 Nov 20 14:11 /mnt/lustre/abc
[root@rocky801 lustre]# md5sum /mnt/lustre/abc
ab893875d697a3145af5eed5309bee26  /mnt/lustre/abc

Specifically, I get that "cannot get UNLOCK lease", does anyone else see that?

Easy copy-paste commands for reproducing:

dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/abc bs=4k count=10 oflag=direct
lfs setstripe -E -1 /mnt/lustre/abc
ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
lfs mirror extend -N /mnt/lustre/abc
ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/abc bs=4k count=1 oflag=direct
ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
md5sum /mnt/lustre/abc
lfs mirror resync /mnt/lustre/abc
ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
lfs mirror delete --mirror-id=1 /mnt/lustre/abc
ls -la /mnt/lustre/abc
md5sum /mnt/lustre/abc

this seem to help:

--- a/lustre/utils/liblustreapi_layout.c
+++ b/lustre/utils/liblustreapi_layout.c
@@ -3523,7 +3523,7 @@ do_read:
         */
        for (i = 0; i < comp_size; i++) {
                comp_array[i].lrc_synced = !comp_array[i].lrc_synced;
-               if (comp_array[i].lrc_synced && pos & (page_size - 1)) {
+               if (comp_array[i].lrc_synced) {
                        rc = llapi_mirror_truncate(fd,
                                        comp_array[i].lrc_mirror_id, pos);
                        /* Ignore truncate error on encrypted file without the
right, so we have 1st replica stale after the 2nd dd. then we resync, but 1st replica's size is not updated properly. this is a bug indeed.

If dd add "conv=notrunc" then the md5sum won't change, w/o that dd would truncate the file size and md5sum would get different values between 40k*0 and 4k*0 buffer.

# dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/40k bs=4k count=10 oflag=direct
10+0 records in
10+0 records out
40960 bytes (41 kB, 40 KiB) copied, 0.051754 s, 791 kB/s

# dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/4k bs=4k count=1 oflag=direct
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
4096 bytes (4.1 kB, 4.0 KiB) copied, 0.0114517 s, 358 kB/s

# md5sum /mnt/lustre/40k
ab893875d697a3145af5eed5309bee26  /mnt/lustre/40k

# md5sum /mnt/lustre/4k
620f0b67a91f7f74151bc5be745b7110  /mnt/lustre/4k

the 2nd dd has count=1 so the checksum is supposed to be different.
then mirror resync seem to "forget" to truncate as file's size is back to 40K after resync.
on the master branch?
